quiz.question:
Black attacks our queen on f4. 

There is an opportunity to play Nf6 fork, to simplify the position.

Is this a good idea, or is there something better?

answers.text: It is not worth it. We should simply step away with the queen, to f3 for example, and keep the pressure.

feedback.text: Good assessment. Suppose we go for the queen trade. the d4-pawn falls at the end and Black's two rooks are pretty powerful.

distractors.text: Let's go for it! The bishop pair will be much more powerful than Black's pair of rooks in the endgame.

feedback.text: White should go for something more conservative. Since this is an endgame, the bishop pair isn't as powerful as it looks against Black's two rooks.
